title: dichrostachys cinerea subsp. keniensis
intro: Dichrostachys cinerea subsp. keniensis, also known as the Kenyan fork bush, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, characterized by its forked branches and small, yellowish flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities, which are often attributed to its high content of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Dichrostachys cinerea subsp. keniensis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating gastrointestinal problems and skin conditions. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
